name: Intune Win32 Retry Reset Script
description: PowerShell tool for Microsoft Intune that forces an immediate retry of a failed Win32 app deployment by clearing the local IME state for the target AppId and restarting the Intune Management Extension service. It does not uninstall the app or modify files; it only resets local Intune state to bypass the Global Re-evaluation Schedule (GRS) and prompt another install attempt. Includes a WhatIf preview and logs to C:\ProgramData\IME-DeepReset-<AppId>.log.
author: Faith Turkoglu

securityCheck.aiSummary: The script is an admin utility intended to deep-clean Intune IME registry traces for a specified Win32 AppId and then restart the Intune Management Extension service. It does not exhibit remote code execution, credential theft, data exfiltration, or obfuscated payloads. The primary security considerations are the potentially destructive registry deletions and service restart actions, which could disrupt Intune functionality if misconfigured. Use -WhatIf for verification, ensure AppId is set correctly, and apply standard change-management controls.
